page.title: Azimut 58 Flybridge: Best Boat in Marbella for Luxury day charter or 3-cabin overnight

data.meta_description: Is the Azimut 58 Flybridge the best boat in Marbella? Compare its 17.6m length, 12-pax capacity & 3-cabin layout for luxury day charters or overnight stays from Puerto Banús.
data.summary: A detailed guide to the Azimut 58 Flybridge, comparing its 17.6m length, 12-pax capacity, and 3-cabin layout for luxury day charters or overnight stays from Puerto Banús. Includes key facts, pricing, and FAQs.
key_facts: Length: 17.6m (58ft) – flybridge layout with sun pads and dining area.
Capacity: 12 passengers – ideal for groups or family charters.
Cabins: 3 en-suite cabins – sleeps 6 guests overnight.
Departure: Puerto Banús, Marbella – direct access to the Costa del Sol.
Pricing: €749/2h to €2,299/8h – includes skipper, drinks, snacks, and VAT.
Inclusions: Licensed skipper, fuel, soft drinks, beer, cava, insurance.
Flybridge: Upper deck with sun loungers and bimini shade.
Overnight option: 3-cabin layout allows multi-day cruising to Estepona or Sotogrande.

faq.q: How many people can the Azimut 58 Flybridge hold?
faq.a: The Azimut 58 Flybridge accommodates up to 12 passengers for day charters. For overnight stays, it sleeps 6 guests across 3 en-suite cabins.

faq.q: What is the price to charter the Azimut 58 Flybridge?
faq.a: Prices start at €749 for 2 hours and go up to €2,299 for 8 hours. This includes a licensed skipper, fuel, soft drinks, beer, cava, snacks, insurance, and 21% VAT.

faq.q: Where does the Azimut 58 Flybridge depart from?
faq.a: All charters depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella. This gives you quick access to the Costa del Sol coastline, including Cabopino, Estepona, and Sotogrande.

faq.q: Can I stay overnight on the Azimut 58 Flybridge?
faq.a: Yes. The yacht has 3 en-suite cabins, sleeping up to 6 guests. Overnight charters are available for multi-day trips along the coast.

faq.q: What is included in the charter price?
faq.a: Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%).

faq.q: Is the Azimut 58 Flybridge suitable for families?
faq.a: Absolutely. The flybridge has sun loungers and shade, while the main deck offers a saloon and dining area. The 3-cabin layout works well for families with children.

faq.q: What destinations can I visit on a day charter?
faq.a: Popular routes include Cala del Faro, Cala Cortés, Cabopino, and Estepona. Longer charters can reach Sotogrande or the Marbella Golden Mile coastline.

faq.q: How does the Azimut 58 compare to other boats in your fleet?
faq.a: The Azimut 58 Flybridge offers a balance of space and performance. It's larger than the Astondoa 40 but smaller than the Mangusta 80. The flybridge gives it a unique upper-deck lounge area.

data.body_html:
The Azimut 58 Flybridge is one of the most versatile yachts in our Marbella fleet. At 17.6 metres, it offers the space of a large motor yacht with the comfort of a flybridge lounge. Whether you want a day charter from Puerto Banús or an overnight cruise to Estepona, this boat delivers. It sleeps 6 guests in 3 en-suite cabins, making it one of the best boats in Marbella for a luxury day charter or 3-cabin overnight. Pricing starts at €749 for 2 hours and rises to €2,299 for 8 hours — all inclusive. Let's break down why the Azimut 58 Flybridge might be the best choice for your next Marbella charter.

Azimut 58 Flybridge: Specs and Layout

The Azimut 58 Flybridge measures 17.6 metres in length and can carry up to 12 passengers. The flybridge itself is a standout feature: a raised upper deck with sun loungers, a dining table, and a bimini shade. Below, the main deck has a saloon with seating, a galley, and a helm station. The lower deck houses 3 en-suite cabins — a master, a VIP, and a twin — each with air conditioning and storage. This layout makes the Azimut 58 one of the best boats in Marbella for groups who want both social space and private sleeping quarters.

Day Charter: What You Get for €749–€2,299

For a day charter, the Azimut 58 Flybridge is priced at €749 for 2 hours, €1,199 for 4 hours, €1,699 for 6 hours, and €2,299 for 8 hours. Every booking includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). You depart from Puerto Banús and can head to Cala del Faro, Cala Cortés, or Cabopino. The flybridge gives you panoramic views while cruising at 25–30 knots. No hidden extras — just show up and enjoy.

Overnight Charters: 3 Cabins for 6 Guests

The Azimut 58 Flybridge is one of the few boats in our fleet with dedicated overnight accommodation. The 3 cabins sleep 6 guests, and the galley includes a fridge, hob, and microwave. Overnight charters are priced on request via WhatsApp, but typical routes include a sunset cruise to Estepona, anchoring overnight, and returning the next day via Sotogrande. The flybridge becomes a stargazing lounge after dark. If you want a weekend escape from Marbella, this is one of the best boats in Marbella for an overnight charter.

How the Azimut 58 Compares to Other Boats in Our Fleet

Our fleet includes the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax), the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax), and the Ferretti 94 (29m, 12 pax). The Azimut 58 sits in the middle: larger than the Astondoa 40 but more affordable than the Mangusta 80. The flybridge is a key differentiator — neither the Astondoa 40 nor the Azimut 39 have one. If you want a flybridge layout with overnight capability, the Azimut 58 is your best option. For pure speed, the Pershing 46 (14m, 10 pax) is faster, but the Azimut 58 offers more comfort and space.

Best Routes from Puerto Banús

From Puerto Banús, the Azimut 58 Flybridge can reach Cala del Faro in 20 minutes, Cabopino in 30 minutes, and Estepona in 45 minutes. For a full-day charter, head to Sotogrande (1 hour) and anchor near the marina. The flybridge gives you a shaded perch to watch the coastline. The 3-cabin layout means you can also do a multi-day trip: day 1 to Estepona, overnight, day 2 to Sotogrande, overnight, day 3 back to Marbella. This flexibility makes the Azimut 58 one of the best boats in Marbella for exploring the Costa del Sol.

Why the Azimut 58 Flybridge Is the Best Boat for Groups

With 12 passengers on a day charter, the Azimut 58 Flybridge has enough space for everyone. The flybridge seats 6–8, the saloon seats 6–8, and the foredeck has sun pads for 4. The galley keeps drinks and snacks flowing. The 3 cabins mean you can split into groups for overnight stays. Compared to the Bandido (11.5m, 8 pax) or the Red Tide (11m, 6 pax), the Azimut 58 is in a different league for group charters. It's one of the best boats in Marbella for birthdays, family reunions, or corporate events.

Pricing and Booking

Day charters on the Azimut 58 Flybridge cost €749 (2h), €1,199 (4h), €1,699 (6h), or €2,299 (8h). Overnight charters are priced on request via WhatsApp. All prices include the skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and 21% VAT. To book, contact us via WhatsApp or email. We recommend booking at least 48 hours in advance, especially during July and August. The Azimut 58 is one of our most popular models, so early booking is advised.

Frequently asked questions

How many people can the Azimut 58 Flybridge hold?

The Azimut 58 Flybridge accommodates up to 12 passengers for day charters. For overnight stays, it sleeps 6 guests across 3 en-suite cabins.

What is the price to charter the Azimut 58 Flybridge?

Prices start at €749 for 2 hours and go up to €2,299 for 8 hours. This includes a licensed skipper, fuel, soft drinks, beer, cava, snacks, insurance, and 21% VAT.

Where does the Azimut 58 Flybridge depart from?

All charters depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella. This gives you quick access to the Costa del Sol coastline, including Cabopino, Estepona, and Sotogrande.

Can I stay overnight on the Azimut 58 Flybridge?

Yes. The yacht has 3 en-suite cabins, sleeping up to 6 guests. Overnight charters are available for multi-day trips along the coast.

What is included in the charter price?

Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%).

Is the Azimut 58 Flybridge suitable for families?

Absolutely. The flybridge has sun loungers and shade, while the main deck offers a saloon and dining area. The 3-cabin layout works well for families with children.

What destinations can I visit on a day charter?

Popular routes include Cala del Faro, Cala Cortés, Cabopino, and Estepona. Longer charters can reach Sotogrande or the Marbella Golden Mile coastline.

How does the Azimut 58 compare to other boats in your fleet?

The Azimut 58 Flybridge offers a balance of space and performance. It's larger than the Astondoa 40 but smaller than the Mangusta 80. The flybridge gives it a unique upper-deck lounge area.
